raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2200 would enact a comprehensive framework of governance, benefits, program standards, and health care cost controls for a single-payer health care coverage system in California.",
    "The status quo of health care access is connected to wealth, employment, and locality, leaving traditionally disadvantaged communities at greater risk.",
    "AB 2200 will start the necessary process of revolutionizing health care access in California.",
    "CalCare will ensure that public health—not profit—is the priority of the health care system.",
    "CalCare will ensure that all Californians can get the health care they need, free at the point of service.",
    "CalCare includes long-term care services and supports for people with disabilities and the elderly.",
    "A single-payer system will cost less overall than the current system and will significantly slow the growth of health care costs, saving billions each year.",
    "Californians overwhelmingly support the transition to a single-payer health system.",
    "In a 2021 poll, 65 percent of low-income Californians supported single-payer health care, with 72 percent support among low-income communities of color."
  ]
}
```
